summary: Russia launched a massive missile and drone strike on Kiev, Dnipro, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian casualties and damage to the Ukroboronprom building. Ukrainian forces reported extensive UAV activity across multiple fronts, with several Shahed attacks intercepted near Kyiv and other regions. Russia also announced the use of high‑precision, long‑range hypersonic weapons against defence‑industrial targets in several oblasts.
key_points: Massive missile and drone strike hit Kiev, Dnipro, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, killing at least 4 and injuring 58 in Kyiv alone (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
Russia employed hypersonic missiles against defence‑industrial facilities in Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Dnipro, Poltava, Khmelnytskyi and Sumy regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Ukrainian air defence reported over 700 aerial attack means used by Russia in a single night, with dozens of UAVs shot down (Source: @kpszsu/63699).

Shahed drones were downed near Lukyanivka, Borispol, Vyshorod, and over the Kyiv sea, indicating continued low‑altitude attack pressure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67426, @mon1tor_ua/67448, @mon1tor_ua/67459, @mon1tor_ua/67457).

Multiple UAV groups were observed moving north‑south and east‑west across the front lines, including groups heading from the south to Zaporizhzhia and from the north of Rivne toward Rokytne (Source: @kpszsu/63713, @kpszsu/63676).
A Ukrainian defence‑industry building (Ukroboronprom) in Kyiv was struck and set on fire during the night strike (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30607).
Russia confirmed the relocation of two Tu‑95MS aircraft that participated in a Far East strike, noting no armed Tu‑95MS or Tu‑160 aircraft remain (Source: @eRadarrua/95020).

sections.executive_summary: Massive missile and drone strike hit Kiev, Dnipro, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian casualties and igniting the Ukroboronprom building. Russian forces used hypersonic missiles against defence‑industrial targets across several oblasts. Ukrainian air defence engaged over 700 aerial attack means, shooting down dozens of UAVs while Shahed drones continued to be intercepted near Kyiv.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported 4 dead and 58 injured in Kyiv after the night missile barrage, with residential buildings and infrastructure hit (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
The Ukroboronprom building in Kyiv was set ablaze by Russian strikes, indicating a direct attack on defence‑industry assets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30607).
Air defence units claimed to have shot down dozens of UAVs and engaged over 700 aerial attack means across the country during the same night (Source: @kpszsu/63699).

Shahed drones were intercepted over Lukyanivka, Borispol, Vyshorod and the Kyiv sea, showing persistent low‑altitude threats to the capital region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67426, @mon1tor_ua/67448, @mon1tor_ua/67459, @mon1tor_ua/67457).
Ukrainian forces reported continued UAV incursions on the northern front, with groups from Rivne heading toward Rokytne and from Chernihiv toward Slavutych (Source: @kpszsu/63676, @kpszsu/63697).
In the south, a group of UAVs from the south was observed moving toward Zaporizhzhia, indicating pressure on that front (Source: @kpszsu/63713).

Ukrainian officials highlighted that Russian hypersonic weapons targeted defence‑industrial sites in multiple oblasts, raising concerns about critical infrastructure vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).

Russia announced the use of high‑precision, long‑range hypersonic missiles against defence‑industrial complexes in Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Dnipro, Poltava, Khmelnytskyi and Sumy regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Russian military statements indicated that no Tu‑95MS or Tu‑160 aircraft are currently armed, but potential threats remain from Tu‑22M3 and MiG‑31K platforms (Source: @eRadarrua/95020).
A combined strike involving drones and missiles hit Kiev, Dnipro, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
Russia reported that over 700 aerial attack means were employed in a single night, reflecting a high‑intensity air campaign (Source: @kpszsu/63699).

Russian defence ministry confirmed the deployment of Shahed drones in multiple directions, including north‑bound attacks on Kyiv and south‑bound attacks on other regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67426, @mon1tor_ua/67448).
TASS reported temporary fuel sales limits in LNR and fuel normalization in Crimea, indicating logistical measures on the occupied territories (Source: @tass_agency/378686, @tass_agency/378678).
Russian aviation authority limited Azur Air's operating certificate until 8 June due to regulatory violations (Source: @tass_agency/378681).
UAV groups were observed moving from the east toward Izium in Kharkiv region, suggesting reconnaissance or strike intent (Source: @kpszsu/63703).
Shahed attacks were reported over Dnipro, with civilian casualties including 6 dead and 36 injured (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
Russian missile strikes hit energy facilities in Dnipro, contributing to power outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian forces reported firefights in the Shostka district of Sumy direction, advancing up to 500 m in several sections (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
A Russian UAV attack set fire to the Ilskoy refinery in Krasnodar region, indicating cross‑border strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian air defence engaged multiple UAVs over the eastern front, with several downed near Kharkiv and Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/63699).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Sumy region, adding to the aerial threat (Source: @kpszsu/63708).
UAV groups from the north of Rivne moved toward Rokytne, indicating pressure on the northern axis (Source: @kpszsu/63676).
UAVs were spotted from the north of Kyiv region near Krasylivka, suggesting reconnaissance over the capital's hinterland (Source: @eRadarrua/95017).
Shahed drones were intercepted over Kyiv from the north, including a strike near Vyshorod (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67459).
UAV activity was reported from the north of Chernihiv heading toward Slavutych, showing continued northern incursions (Source: @kpszsu/63697).
Russian air defence exercised over the north, with reports of over 700 aerial attack means used nationwide (Source: @kpszsu/63699).
A Shahed drone was downed over Borispol, confirming northern approach vectors (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67448).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV groups moving westward across the northern front, including from Rivne toward the west (Source: @kpszsu/63682).
UAV groups from the south moved toward Zaporizhzhia, indicating pressure on the southern industrial hub (Source: @kpszsu/63713).
Russian hypersonic missiles targeted defence‑industrial sites in Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv, highlighting southern strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Shahed attacks were reported over Dnipro, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
Ukrainian air defence reported downing multiple UAVs over the southern front, including near Dnipro and Kherson directions (Source: @kpszsu/63699).
Russian forces used combined drone and missile strikes on southern cities, including an energy facility hit in Dnipro (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
UAV activity was noted from the Black Sea towards Odesa, suggesting maritime‑to‑land threat vectors (Source: @kpszsu/63698).
Ukrainian reports indicated that Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Sumy, but similar operations were observed on the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/63708).
Russia employed over 700 aerial attack means in a single night, including missiles, drones and Shahed loitering munitions (Source: @kpszsu/63699).

Ukrainian air defence intercepted multiple Shahed drones over Kyiv, Borispol and the Kyiv sea, demonstrating active low‑altitude defence (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67426, @mon1tor_ua/67448, @mon1tor_ua/67457).
Russian hypersonic missiles were used against defence‑industrial targets across seven oblasts, indicating a strategic escalation in missile capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Ukrainian forces reported downing dozens of UAVs nationwide, with engagements reported in the north, south, east and central regions (Source: @kpszsu/63699).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Sumy region, expanding the use of precision air‑to‑ground weapons (Source: @kpszsu/63708).
No armed Tu‑95MS or Tu‑160 aircraft remain in Russian inventory after relocation, but Tu‑22M3 and MiG‑31K pose ongoing threats (Source: @eRadarrua/95020).
Ukrainian air defence reported a failure to intercept a massive missile wave over Kyiv, citing poor performance and reliance on partner support (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30603).

key_developments: Russia launched a massive missile and drone strike on Kiev, Dnipro, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian casualties and igniting the Ukroboronprom building (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
Russian forces employed hypersonic missiles against defence‑industrial facilities in Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Dnipro, Poltava, Khmelnytskyi and Sumy regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Ukrainian air defence reported over 700 aerial attack means used by Russia in a single night, with dozens of UAVs shot down across the country (Source: @kpszsu/63699).

Shahed drones were intercepted near Lukyanivka, Borispol, Vyshorod and over the Kyiv sea, indicating continued low‑altitude attack pressure on the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67426, @mon1tor_ua/67448, @mon1tor_ua/67459, @mon1tor_ua/67457).

Ukrainian UAV groups were observed moving from the south toward Zaporizhzhia and from the north of Rivne toward Rokytne, showing active aerial reconnaissance and strike attempts on multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/63713, @kpszsu/63676).

A Ukrainian defence‑industry building (Ukroboronprom) in Kiev was struck and set on fire during the night attack, highlighting a targeted strike on military production capacity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30607).
Russia confirmed the relocation of two Tu‑95MS aircraft that participated in a Far East strike, noting that no armed Tu‑95MS or Tu‑160 aircraft remain in service (Source: @eRadarrua/95020).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian operations demonstrate a coordinated escalation, combining high‑precision hypersonic missiles, large‑scale UAV swarms and Shahed loitering munitions to target both civilian infrastructure and defence‑industrial assets. Ukrainian air defence is strained, reporting over 700 attack vectors in a single night and mixed success against low‑altitude threats. Continued UAV movements across all fronts suggest persistent reconnaissance and strike intent, while the removal of armed strategic bombers indicates a shift toward more flexible, shorter‑range strike platforms.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine’s military reports extensive casualties and infrastructure damage, particularly in Kyiv, Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia, prompting heightened civil defence alerts and increased air‑defence deployments. International partners are being urged to improve air‑defence support, as Ukrainian statements cite poor performance against the recent missile wave. Russian statements emphasize the use of advanced weaponry and logistical measures in occupied territories, indicating a broader strategic posture.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths reveals consistent pressure on northern, southern and eastern axes, requiring real‑time interception capabilities. The deployment of hypersonic missiles against multiple oblasts suggests a focus on crippling Ukraine’s defence‑industrial output. The absence of armed Tu‑95MS/Tu‑160 bombers may limit long‑range strategic bombing but increases reliance on tactical aircraft and missile systems. Continued analysis of Russian fuel and logistics statements may provide insight into sustainment of prolonged operations.
